speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Knights,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: To say you’re welcome were superfluous.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: To place upon the volume of your deeds,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: As in a title page, your worth in arms

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Were more than you expect or more than ’s fit,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Since every worth in show commends itself.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Prepare for mirth, for mirth becomes a feast.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: You are princes and my guests.

speaker: THAISA
text: But you my knight and guest,

speaker: THAISA
text: To whom this wreath of victory I give

speaker: THAISA
text: And crown you king of this day’s happiness.

speaker: PERICLES
text: ’Tis more by fortune, lady, than my merit.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Call it by what you will, the day is yours,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And here, I hope, is none that envies it.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: In framing an artist, Art hath thus decreed,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: To make some good but others to exceed,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And you are her labored scholar.—Come, queen o’ the feast,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: For, daughter, so you are; here, take your place.—

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Marshal, the rest as they deserve their grace.

speaker: KNIGHTS
text: We are honored much by good Simonides.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Your presence glads our days. Honor we love,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: For who hates honor hates the gods above.

speaker: MARSHAL
text: Sir, yonder is your place.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Some other is more fit.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              KNIGHT
text: Contend not, sir, for we are gentlemen

speaker:
FIRST
               
              KNIGHT
text: Have neither in our hearts nor outward eyes

speaker:
FIRST
               
              KNIGHT
text: Envies the great, nor shall the low despise.

speaker: PERICLES
text: You are right courteous knights.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Sit, sir, sit.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text:
Aside
                .
                 
              By Jove I wonder, that is king of thoughts,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: These cates resist me, he not thought upon.

speaker: THAISA
text: By Juno, that is queen of marriage,

speaker: THAISA
text: All viands that I eat do seem unsavory,

speaker: THAISA
text: Wishing him my meat.—Sure, he’s a gallant gentleman.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: He’s but a country gentleman;

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Has done no more than other knights have done;

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Has broken a staff or so. So let it pass.

speaker: THAISA
text: To me he seems like diamond to glass.

speaker: PERICLES
text: Yon king’s to me like to my father’s picture,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Which tells in that glory once he was—

speaker: PERICLES
text: Had princes sit like stars about his throne,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And he the sun for them to reverence.

speaker: PERICLES
text: None that beheld him but like lesser lights

speaker: PERICLES
text: Did vail their crowns to his supremacy;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Where now his son’s like a glowworm in the night,

speaker: PERICLES
text: The which hath fire in darkness, none in light;

speaker: PERICLES
text: Whereby I see that Time’s the king of men.

speaker: PERICLES
text: He’s both their parent, and he is their grave,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And gives them what he will, not what they crave.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: What, are you merry, knights?

speaker: KNIGHTS
text: Who can be other in this royal presence?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Here, with a cup that’s stored unto the brim,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: As do you love, fill to your mistress’ lips.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: We drink this health to you.

speaker: KNIGHTS
text: We thank your Grace.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Yet pause awhile. Yon knight doth sit too melancholy,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: As if the entertainment in our court

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Had not a show might countervail his worth.—

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Note it not you, Thaisa?

speaker: THAISA
text: What is ’t to me, my father?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: O, attend, my daughter. Princes in this

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Should live like gods above, who freely give

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: To everyone that come to honor them.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And princes not doing so are like to gnats,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Which make a sound but, killed, are wondered at.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Therefore, to make his entrance more sweet,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Here, say we drink this standing-bowl of wine to him.

speaker: THAISA
text: Alas, my father, it befits not me

speaker: THAISA
text: Unto a stranger knight to be so bold.

speaker: THAISA
text: He may my proffer take for an offense,

speaker: THAISA
text: Since men take women’s gifts for impudence.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: How?

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Do as I bid you, or you’ll move me else.

speaker: THAISA
text: Now, by the gods, he could not please me better.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And furthermore tell him we desire to know of him

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Of whence he is, his name and parentage.

speaker: THAISA
text: The King, my father, sir, has drunk to you.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I thank him.

speaker: THAISA
text: Wishing it so much blood unto your life.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I thank both him and you, and pledge him freely.

speaker: THAISA
text: And further, he desires to know of you

speaker: THAISA
text: Of whence you are, your name and parentage.

speaker: PERICLES
text: A gentleman of Tyre, my name Pericles.

speaker: PERICLES
text: My education been in arts and arms,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Who, looking for adventures in the world,

speaker: PERICLES
text: Was by the rough seas reft of ships and men,

speaker: PERICLES
text: And after shipwrack driven upon this shore.

speaker: THAISA
text: He thanks your Grace; names himself Pericles,

speaker: THAISA
text: A gentleman of Tyre,

speaker: THAISA
text: Who only by misfortune of the seas,

speaker: THAISA
text: Bereft of ships and men, cast on this shore.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Now, by the gods, I pity his misfortune,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And will awake him from his melancholy.—

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Come, gentlemen, we sit too long on trifles

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And waste the time which looks for other revels.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Even in your armors, as you are addressed,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Will well become a soldiers’ dance.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: I will not have excuse with saying this:

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: “Loud music is too harsh for ladies’ heads,”

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Since they love men in arms as well as beds.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: So, this was well asked, ’twas so well performed.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Come, sir.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Here’s a lady that wants breathing too,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And I have heard you knights of Tyre

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Are excellent in making ladies trip,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And that their measures are as excellent.

speaker: PERICLES
text: In those that practice them they are, my lord.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: O, that’s as much as you would be denied

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Of your fair courtesy.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Unclasp, unclasp!

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Thanks, gentlemen, to all; all have done well;

speaker: SIMONIDES
text:
To
                 
                Pericles
                .
                 
              But you the best.—Pages and lights, to conduct

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: These knights unto their several lodgings.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Yours, sir,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: We have given order be next our own.

speaker: PERICLES
text: I am at your Grace’s pleasure.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Princes, it is too late to talk of love,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: And that’s the mark I know you level at.

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Therefore each one betake him to his rest,

speaker: SIMONIDES
text: Tomorrow all for speeding do their best.
